critic_raw:
Preprocessing is fit on the entire dataset before splitting — all normalization, TF-IDF vocabulary, and image normalization (including DICOM whole-set statistics) include test-set information.

ResNet-50 is fine-tuned during training (described as 'fine-tuned during training yielding 2048-dimensional embeddings') while the text branch uses static TF-IDF vectorization with no fine-tuning. This architectural asymmetry means the image branch has task-specific learning capacity while the text branch does not. The multi-modal model effectively has a fine-tuned visual encoder and a frozen text encoder, which is not a balanced architecture — it could systematically underweight text in the fusion.

Disease labels from ICD-10 codes are administrative codes assigned by coders, not gold-standard clinical diagnoses. ICD-10 coding is known to have specificity issues — codes may be assigned for rule-out diagnoses, billing optimization, or based on provisional assessments. Using administrative codes as ground truth means the model is evaluated against a noisy, potentially systematically biased label that underrepresents rare diseases and over-represents common billing codes.

The disease taxonomy is completely unspecified. The experiment is a 'multi-class classification' with unknown number of classes, unknown class prevalence, and unknown clinical domain. A model that works well for imaging-defined diseases (pneumonia, fractures) will produce radically different results than one applied to metabolic or psychiatric conditions where images are uninformative. Without specifying the disease set, the experimental claim is not reproducible or interpretable.

Clinician/department is included as both a stratification variable in the split and as a feature. Including clinician ID as a model feature encodes practice-pattern shortcuts — a clinician who predominantly treats a specific disease creates a spurious association between clinician identity and diagnosis. This reduces to memorizing department-disease associations rather than learning disease-level features, which will not generalize to new clinicians.
all_issues_raised: Preprocessing including DICOM normalization statistics, TF-IDF vocabulary, and feature scaling are fit on the entire dataset before splitting — test-set image and text statistics inform training transformations.

ResNet-50 image branch is fine-tuned during training while TF-IDF text branch is static — this architectural asymmetry gives the image branch task-specific learning capacity that the text branch lacks, creating unbalanced fusion.

Disease labels are derived from ICD-10 administrative codes, which have known specificity problems due to provisional diagnoses, billing optimization, and coding conventions — the model is trained and evaluated against systematically noisy ground truth.

The disease taxonomy (number of classes, class prevalence, clinical domains) is entirely unspecified — a claim about multi-modal vs. text-only performance without specifying the disease space is not interpretable or reproducible.

Clinician ID is included as a model feature — this creates department-disease association shortcuts that do not generalize to new clinicians and reduce the model to memorizing practice patterns rather than learning diagnostic features.
all_issues_adjudicated: Preprocessing including DICOM normalization statistics, TF-IDF vocabulary, and feature scaling are fit on the entire dataset before splitting — test-set image and text statistics inform training transformations.

ResNet-50 image branch is fine-tuned during training while TF-IDF text branch is static — this architectural asymmetry gives the image branch task-specific learning capacity that the text branch lacks, creating unbalanced fusion.

Disease labels are derived from ICD-10 administrative codes, which have known specificity problems due to provisional diagnoses, billing optimization, and coding conventions — the model is trained and evaluated against systematically noisy ground truth.

The disease taxonomy (number of classes, class prevalence, clinical domains) is entirely unspecified — a claim about multi-modal vs. text-only performance without specifying the disease space is not interpretable or reproducible.

Clinician ID is included as a model feature — this creates department-disease association shortcuts that do not generalize to new clinicians and reduce the model to memorizing practice patterns rather than learning diagnostic features.
